compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review round-3 advisory triage: the finding asking for a live-model inference AC (prove the new rubric text fires on R-non-gating-skip / R-fault-wrong-seam and stays silent on the FP cases) is judged INVALID AS SCOPED and not adopted. Rationale: the repo's eval runner (rebar prompt eval) is offline validate+summarize only — there is no live-inference harness in the CLI or CI; the established convention for standing eval specs (story f93a) is strict offline validation in CI plus separate, billable experiment runs outside the commit gate. Adding a live-inference AC would make the ticket unclosable in CI. The offline validation AC (validate_eval_spec strict over the four new cases) is retained; a live experiment run remains available as the documented follow-on outside this ticket.

compiled_state.description:
## What

Extend `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md` (the Pass-1 test-sufficiency overlay) with the two meaningful antipatterns the current rubric does not name, each paired with explicit false-positive guards so the addition catches real deficiencies without nits or over-firing — and extend `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml` with recall + no-fire cases so the eval suite owns the new rubric surface. The overlay file is the runtime rubric the model consults during Pass-1, so new antipatterns must be added there (not only to the eval dataset) to be effective at inference time; the existing single-file structure avoids any routing change. Prompt style: the overlay is a DETECTOR rubric, so naming antipatterns is its function — additions stay in the existing rubric's terse litmus style (one bolded name, one-sentence definition, one-line litmus), addressed to the reviewing LLM, with no elaborated misbehavior narratives beyond what recognition requires. No front-matter, posture, threshold, or routing change (the criterion stays advisory; `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` untouched).

Antipatterns to add to the FLAG list:
- **non-gating test**: a new/changed test that silently skips or non-strict-xfails under the project's CI — the test provides no regression protection because it never runs where it gates. Two condition classes, judged differently: an UNCONDITIONAL suppression (a bare `skip`, `skipif(True)`/constant-true condition, or a non-strict `xfail` with no environment dependence) is flaggable from the diff alone — no CI context needed. An ENVIRONMENT-DEPENDENT condition (platform/dependency/env-var guards) is judged against the project's CI context readable with the reviewer's file tools (CI workflow files, the Makefile's test target); when that context is not determinable, do NOT flag (fail-open applies ONLY to this class). Guards: a skip guarding an optional platform/dependency that the CI context does exercise is NOT a finding; a strict xfail (`strict=True`) whose `reason=` string (or an adjacent comment/linked ticket) names a concrete removal condition is NOT a finding — a bare non-strict xfail, or a strict one with no stated reason, IS flaggable.
- **unrealistic fault injection / sleep-only synchronization**: (a) a recovery/cleanup/rollback path 'tested' by injecting the fault OUTSIDE the seam under test (e.g. replacing the top-level command with a raiser when the contract is mid-operation rollback), so the recovery code never executes; (b) a concurrency/race test whose primary synchronization is a timing sleep. Guards: a fault injected at the real boundary (monkeypatching the actual failing call at the point the contract covers) is VALID; a short sleep used as a secondary settling wait alongside a deterministic primary mechanism (barrier, pipe, marker file, event) is NOT a finding.

Eval coverage to add in `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` (keeping the recall/no-fire families balanced per `test_code_review_dataset_balanced` — if that test requires parity, add one further no-fire case, e.g. a test that HARD-FAILS on a missing required executable instead of skipping, which the rubric explicitly endorses):
- recall `R-non-gating-skip` (mode: non-gating-test): a diff adding a test under an UNCONDITIONAL suppression (e.g. `@pytest.mark.skip(reason="flaky")` or `skipif(True)`), flaggable from the diff alone so the case cannot be suppressed by the fail-open guard (the eval solver sends diffs inline without a fixture repo).
- recall `R-sleep-only-race` (mode: unrealistic-fault-injection): a diff adding a concurrency test whose only synchronization is `time.sleep`, asserting shared state afterwards.
- recall `R-fault-wrong-seam` (mode: unrealistic-fault-injection): a diff testing rollback by monkeypatching the top-level command to raise, so the rollback loop never runs.
- no-fire `FP-strict-xfail-with-reason` (mode: strict-xfail-valid): a strict xfail with a reason naming a removal condition.
- no-fire `FP-sleep-as-secondary` (mode: sleep-secondary-valid): a race test synchronized by a barrier/marker file with a short settling sleep.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each antipattern sits in the FLAG list and each guard set in the false-positive-guards section (section-scoped proof, not a bare file grep). Prove: `awk '/Antipatterns to FLAG/,/False-positive GUARDS/' src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md | grep -qi 'non-gating'` and the same for 'fault injection'; `awk '/False-positive GUARDS/,/evidence-record contract/' src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md | grep -qi 'strict.*xfail'` and the same for 'settling'. Phrasing is litmus-style consistent with the existing rubric.
- [ ] Each new eval case id is present. Prove: `for id in R-non-gating-skip R-sleep-only-race R-fault-wrong-seam FP-strict-xfail-with-reason FP-sleep-as-secondary; do grep -q "$id" src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml || { echo "MISSING $id"; exit 1; }; done`.
- [ ] Front-matter unchanged. Prove: `python -m rebar.llm.prompting.prompts regenerate-index 2>/dev/null || python -m rebar.llm.prompts regenerate-index` then `git diff --exit-code -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/index.json` (the same primary-then-fallback pair CI's prompt-index gate runs).
- [ ] `git diff` shows no change to `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E5.md`, `criteria_routing.json` (either gate), or any threshold/posture field (this task owns the epic's no-E5-edit guard).
- [ ] `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_evals.py tests/unit/test_code_review_fp_ledger.py -q` passes with the new eval cases present — including the existing spec-validation tests, which assert every dataset case (the new four included) parses under `validate_eval_spec` (strict) and carries the required fields; the rubric's evidence-record contract section itself is left textually unchanged (prove: `git diff src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md` shows no hunk touching the 'evidence-record contract' block).
- [ ] `make lint` passes.
compiled_state.title: code-review tests criterion: flag non-gating tests and unrealistic fault injection (with FP guards)

verify_commands.command: python -m rebar.llm.prompting.prompts regenerate-index && git diff --exit-code -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/index.json
